marketState: neutral-to-bearish — storage surplus and a broad technical downtrend outweigh a hot-weather demand tailwind
summary: Front price ($2.851) sits almost exactly at model fair value now (-$0.072 vs $2.923) but the fair-value curve climbs to $3.060 by day 21 while spot keeps drifting to 'slightly under', hinting at a growing gap if the model holds. Fundamentally the picture is mixed: cooling-season demand is running well above the 5-year norm both this week (+18.6%, rangePos 0.79) and 8-16 days out (+15.3%, rangePos 0.87), yet storage still built +41 Bcf to a +180 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average and beat the forecast (bearish +22 Bcf surprise) — strong AC demand hasn't stopped the glut from growing. Seasonality is weak and unreliable (Low, 59pp 10-90 spread) but tilts slightly bearish for 3-4 weeks (only 38% odds higher, -1.4% typical move), consistent with managed money still net short (-60,295 contracts, 35th percentile — room to extend). All six technical timeframes show a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses, though RSI readings of 32-37 and a sub-lower-Bollinger 4h close suggest the selloff is stretched and vulnerable to a near-term bounce. Net: the medium-term fundamental backdrop favors fading strength, while the near-term signal is too conflicted (oversold/bearish trend vs bullish weather) for a clean directional call.

longTerm.rationale: Storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average and just posted a bearish surprise (+22 Bcf vs +19 forecast) despite above-normal cooling demand, signaling persistent oversupply into the shoulder season; seasonality (Low reliability) still tilts bearish for weeks 3-4 (38% odds higher, -1.4% typical move) and managed money remains net short with room to add (35th percentile).
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.20-3.30, or a string of bullish (below-forecast) storage builds alongside continued above-normal demand, would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Signals conflict: all six timeframes show bearish trend/EMA structure, but RSI (32-37) and a 4h close below the lower Bollinger band point to an oversold, bounce-prone market, while near-term weather demand (+18.6% vs 5y, rangePos 0.79) is bullish and the futures curve is nearly flat front-to-next. Price is also essentially at fair value now, leaving no clean discount/premium edge to trade.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) bullish confirmation — RSI back above 40-45 with MACD histogram turning positive on 1h/4h and price reclaiming the 4h lower Bollinger band — to play a bounce long, or (b) a clean close below $2.70-2.75 support to confirm downside continuation for a short scalp.

keyDrivers: Storage +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish surprise (+22 Bcf) despite hot weather
Cooling demand +18.6% (1-7D) / +15.3% (8-16D) vs 5y, near top of range
Price ~fair now but fair-value curve rises to $3.060 by +21D while spot drifts to 'slightly under'
All 6 technical timeframes bearish trend/EMA, but RSI 32-37 and 4h sub-lower-Bollinger signal oversold
Managed money net short -60,295 contracts (35th percentile) — room to extend, not extreme
risks: Weather forecasts can flip quickly, erasing the demand tailwind that's currently offsetting storage builds
Oversold technicals (RSI, Bollinger) could trigger a sharp short-covering bounce against the short thesis
Low seasonality reliability (59pp 10-90 spread) means the historical seasonal edge is weak this week
